title: Passer Mortuus Est
content:
Death devours all lovely things;
                 Lesbia with her sparrow
             Shares the darkness,--presently
                 Every bed is narrow.

             Unremembered as old rain
                 Dries the sheer libation,
             And the little petulant hand
                 Is an annotation.

             After all, my erstwhile dear,
                 My no longer cherished,
             Need we say it was not love,
                 Now that love is perished?
meaning: About: Edna St. Vincent Millay was an American lyrical poet and playwright and the first woman to receive the Pulitzer Prize for Poetry. She was also known for her unconventional, bohemian lifestyle and her many love affairs. She used the pseudonym Nancy Boyd for her prose work.
